role_title: Associate Manager, Product Operations GTM - Strategy & Operations

level_signals: Title is 'Associate Manager' suggesting individual contributor with some seniority

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have 4+ years of experience in product operations, go-to-market, business operations, strategy & operations, product marketing, consulting, or a related field
You have experience leading cross-functional workstreams in a high-growth environment
You are highly structured and can turn ambiguity into clear plans, timelines, and deliverables
You are an excellent writer and communicator, able to distill complex product details into simple messaging for internal teams
You have strong product sense and can connect customer needs, product capabilities, and commercial implications
You are comfortable managing many stakeholders and driving alignment without direct authority
You are detail-oriented and operationally rigorous, especially when managing launch timelines, dependencies, and field readiness
You are proactive, scrappy, and energized by building new processes in a fast-moving environment
You are customer-first and bring humility, curiosity, and strong judgment to cross-functional work

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience in SaaS, B2B product launches, enablement, or field-facing GTM roles is a plus

scope.surface: DoorDash Digital Ordering – SaaS product suite for merchants (online ordering, branded mobile apps, loyalty solutions)
scope.business_model: SaaS / B2B — selling first-party digital ordering and loyalty tools to merchants (restaurants, grocers, retailers)
primary_metrics_inferred: Product adoption rates for newly launched features
Field readiness and sales enablement effectiveness
core_responsibilities: Own the GTM operating process for Digital Ordering product launches, including new features, pricing changes, and strategic platform updates
Draft and maintain the core Product GTM Brief for launches
Lead cross-functional project management across Product, PMM, Marketing, Sales Enablement, Sales, and Strategy & Operations to bring products to market successfully
Partner with Marketing to inform launch messaging and channel strategy across email, content, PR, paid, events, and other relevant channels
Partner with Sales Enablement to ensure field readiness through clear enablement planning and materials
Own updates to battlecards and other competitive or field-facing product resources
Serve as the primary point of contact for field teams and sales enablement on product-related questions
Partner with Product to define and track launch success metrics and adoption KRs for newly launched products
Translate field feedback, customer needs, and launch learnings into actionable recommendations for future GTM motions and product improvements
Improve launch processes, templates, and operating rhythms to make Product GTM more scalable as the business grows

green_flags: Clear and specific role scope within a defined business unit (Digital Ordering)
Describes the business as 'one of the fastest-growing segments within DoorDash' — growth opportunity signal
Explicit application deadline (6/13/26) suggests active and time-bound hiring process
Role spans multiple high-visibility functions (Product, Sales, Marketing, Ops) — broad exposure
red_flags: Location type is not clearly stated — role could be hybrid, remote, or onsite; NYC AI/AEDT notice implies possible NYC anchor but is not definitive
Base pay range is wide ($50K spread) suggesting significant variance based on location and experience
No explicit visa sponsorship information provided
Role title 'Associate Manager' may understate seniority expectations (4+ years required, leading XFN workstreams)
